mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration that depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between retail sales momentum and housing starts indicates shifting consumer demand patterns; when retail sales rise while housing starts decline, equity markets price in resilient non-housing consumption, driving positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month changes in industrial production signal improving factory utilization and supply chain normalization, which directly boosts short-term equity returns during early recovery phases.
failure_condition: The signal loses statistical significance with p-values above 0.10 outside recovery regimes or shows negative IC during recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates above 0.8 percent month-over-month while policy rates remain near zero, markets anticipate forced monetary tightening that compresses equity multiples and generates negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of triggered instances despite the inflation signal.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly correlate with rising corporate revenue expectations and consumer spending capacity, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows or the signal reverses direction during expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: The growth differential between retail sales and real GDP captures consumption intensity; when retail sales growth outpaces GDP growth, it signals strong domestic demand pulling the economy forward, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows negative predictive power or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and housing starts below their respective 12-month moving averages indicates a broad-based supply-side contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both below 12-month MA
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, invalidating the contraction signal.
